seoTitle: How to Create Multiple Lines in a Power BI Line Chart
metaDescription: Compare trends across categories by adding multiple lines to a Power BI line chart, with tips on customizing colors and layout.
intro: A single line only tells part of the story, but adding multiple lines to a Power BI chart lets you compare trends across categories like products, regions, or time periods. This guide walks through building and customizing a multi-line chart step by step. It's a core visualization skill for anyone tracking performance metrics over time.

steps.heading: Set up your Power BI screen
steps.body: Prepare the report page and confirm your dataset includes the fields needed to compare multiple categories over time.

steps.heading: Add data fields to your line chart
steps.body: Assign your date field to the axis and add multiple measures or a category field so more than one line appears on the chart.

steps.heading: Customize line styles and colors
steps.body: Adjust the color and style of each line so they're easy to tell apart at a glance.

steps.heading: Compare trends effectively
steps.body: Use the chart to look for patterns, crossovers, or divergences between the lines that reveal meaningful business trends.

steps.heading: Apply it to your reporting process
steps.body: Use multi-line comparisons in real reports, such as tracking sales or expenses across different categories over time.

faq.question: How many lines can I add to a single Power BI chart?
faq.answer: There's no hard limit, but keeping the number of lines manageable helps the chart stay readable rather than becoming cluttered.

faq.question: Can I compare different metrics, not just categories, on the same chart?
faq.answer: Yes, you can plot multiple measures as separate lines, which is useful for comparing different metrics over the same time period.

faq.question: Why do my lines look too similar in color?
faq.answer: Power BI assigns default colors automatically, so you may need to manually adjust the color palette to make each line more distinct.

faq.question: Can I highlight just one line without removing the others?
faq.answer: Yes, formatting options let you emphasize one line, such as with a bolder color, while keeping the others visible for context.

faq.question: Is a multi-line chart good for showing long-term trends?
faq.answer: Yes, it's well suited for spotting long-term trends and comparing how different categories move relative to each other over time.
